div1.entryFree: Scar, vb. to wound, to hurt: England hath long been mad and --ed herself, R3 V, 5, 23. and --ed the moon with splinters, Cor. IV, 5, 115 (Rowe scared; cf. R3 V, 3, 341. According to Nares, to scar was sometimes equivalent to scare. cf. Scar-crow). whose loss hath pierced him deep and --ed his heart, Tit. IV, 4, 31. I'll not shed her blood, nor s. that skin of hers, Oth. V, 2, 4. cf. Unscarred.
